description: In a temperate cycle, 'keloi' follows spring and gives way to autumn. It commonly brings the year's warmest conditions and long daylight, while its ecological and social effects depend on the place.
articulatory_notes: Press the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k' and release into middle-height front 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and draw the tongue back into stressed middle-height rounded 'o'. Unround and raise the front of the tongue for the separate final 'i'.

examples.phi: ha keloi ru waora to nai.

semantic_domains.nature: the warm, long-day portion of a temperate year
semantic_domains.temporal: the season between spring and autumn
